artPrompt: The dramatic moment of a botched purification: a great rusted treatment pipe gushes water cleanly from one half and vomits bubbling green sludge from the other, the two streams meeting and warring in a churning pool below, bioluminescent froth bleeding between clean and foul. Mutant overgrowth creeps over concrete. Wide landscape, murky green and rust-red washes against a bruised sky. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Half-Treated, Half Not
text:
Put half a sludge counter on each creature you control. (Yes, half. Split the difference and argue about it.) A creature with a fractional sludge counter is treated as having the next whole number up whenever that helps you and the next whole number down whenever it helps an opponent.
Draw half a card, rounded up to a whole card because you cannot draw half a card, you tried, it didn't work, stop trying.
flavorText: The plant runs at 50% capacity, which is to say it runs the water through and shrugs.
